export async function runRoutinesMigration(db: Kysely<RoutinesDatabase>): Promise<void> {
  await sql`
    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS routines_v1_definitions (
      agent_id        TEXT        NOT NULL,
      path            TEXT        NOT NULL,
      author_user_id  TEXT        NOT NULL,
      name            TEXT        NOT NULL,
      description     TEXT        NOT NULL,
      spec_hash       TEXT        NOT NULL,
      trigger_kind    TEXT        NOT NULL CHECK (trigger_kind IN ('interval','cron','webhook')),
      trigger_spec    JSONB       NOT NULL,
      active_hours    JSONB,
      silence_token   TEXT,
      silence_max     INTEGER     NOT NULL DEFAULT 300,
      conversation    TEXT        NOT NULL CHECK (conversation IN ('per-fire','shared')),
      prompt_body     TEXT        NOT NULL,
      next_run_at     TIMESTAMPTZ,
      last_run_at     TIMESTAMPTZ,
      last_status     TEXT,
      last_error      TEXT,
      created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
      updated_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
      PRIMARY KEY (agent_id, path)
    )
  `.execute(db);

  await sql`
    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS routines_v1_due
      ON routines_v1_definitions (next_run_at)
     WHERE next_run_at IS NOT NULL
  `.execute(db);

+  // KNOWN LIMITATION: `workspace:read` does not surface the current
+  // workspace version, so we pass `parent: null`. Against the git workspace
+  // backend this will fail with `parent-mismatch` whenever the workspace
+  // already has a commit history (i.e., always, since the routine file
+  // necessarily lives at a non-empty version). The mismatch propagates to
+  // the caller; the @ax/routines silence-token call site is fire-and-forget
+  // and surfaces the error in logs without crashing the routine run.
+  // The correct fix is a workspace contract change: either extend

input.command:
git add -A && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
chore(routines): address PR #71 review feedback

Eight items flagged by CodeRabbit + github-code-quality bot:

Critical
  - packages/routines/package.json: bump kysely 0.27.0 → 0.28.17 to
    match the rest of the monorepo and pull in the JSON-path / MySQL
    backslash / JSON-key-injection security fixes (0.28.12 / 0.28.14 /
    0.28.17). Phase B's plan used the wrong version pin.

Major
  - packages/routines/src/clock.ts: handle pre-aborted AbortSignal in
    `sleep` — addEventListener('abort', …) does not fire if signal
    aborted before the listener attaches; check `signal.aborted`
    first and resolve immediately.
  - packages/routines/src/migrations.ts: tighten DB-side constraints
    — silence_max CHECK (>= 0) and last_status CHECK IN
    ('ok','silenced','error'). App layer already assumes these; the
    constraints stop manual SQL edits and future migrations from
    poisoning the scheduler.
  - packages/routines/src/plugin.ts: do NOT fall back to empty
    `turnId` on the chat:turn-end one-shot handler. The current
    EventTurnEndSchema in @ax/ipc-protocol has no `turnId` field, so
    every chat:turn-end arrives with turnId === undefined; passing
    '' to conversations:drop-turn would silently delete the last
    turn (potentially an unrelated message). Now we log
    `routines_drop_turn_skipped_no_turn_id` and skip drop-turn until
    a turnId mechanism is wired up. The conversation hide still runs
    for per-fire silenced replies, which is what makes the silence
    contract user-visibly correct.
  - packages/conversations/src/plugin.ts: document the `parent: null`
    limitation in drop-turn. Against the git workspace backend this
    will always hit parent-mismatch when a file pre-exists; the
    correct fix is a workspace contract change (extend
    WorkspaceReadOutput with version OR add
    workspace:get-current-version) and touches multiple workspace
    implementations + the wire protocol. Deferred to a follow-up
    PR; the comment surfaces the gap so a future reader doesn't
    spend time re-deriving it.
  - packages/routines/src/__tests__/canary.test.ts: the
    "shared routine reuses the same conversation" test now actually
    verifies reuse — find-or-create mock caches by externalKey,
    captured agent:invoke ctx.conversationId is asserted equal
    across both fires, and we assert find-or-create (not :create)
    was called twice with the routine path as the externalKey.
  - packages/routines/src/__tests__/canary.test.ts +
    service-hooks.test.ts: drop `.catch(() => {})` swallows in DB
    cleanup teardown. If TRUNCATE or client.end() ever fails, the
    test should surface the error rather than silently mask test
    isolation regressions.

Minor
  - packages/conversations/src/__tests__/drop-turn.test.ts: the
    "is a no-op when the conversation has no runnerSessionId" test
    now asserts `getLastApplied()` is undefined — proves the contract
    rather than just non-throw behavior.
  - packages/validator-routine/src/frontmatter.ts: TIME_OF_DAY_RE
    previously accepted 24:01..24:59 as valid times. New regex
    accepts 00:00..23:59 plus exactly 24:00 (the "midnight at end-of-
    day" sentinel used by activeHours.end — see active-hours.ts).

Canary assertion drift
  - The silence-token canary asserted `captured.drops.length === 1`,
    but with the new "skip drop-turn when no turnId" guard the count
    is 0 in the current chat:turn-end wire shape. Updated the
    assertion + added an explanatory comment pointing at
    EventTurnEndSchema.

Not addressed (deferred):
  - CodeRabbit's note about @ax/routines runtime imports from
    @ax/validator-routine. Already resolved in commit 86e1a7b via
    the eslint allowlist update — same precedent as
    @ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner-host, justified inline in
    eslint.config.mjs.
  - CodeRabbit's note about pg_try_advisory_lock + Kysely pool
    connection mismatch. The advisory lock is documented as
    best-effort election; FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED in claimDue is the
    actual correctness guarantee, so two replicas tick-racing each
    other still claim disjoint sets. Pinning a connection via
    db.connection() is a worthwhile follow-up but does not affect
    correctness in Phase B.

`pnpm build` clean; routines 58/58, conversations 120/120,
validator-routine 22/22 green; lint reports 0 errors (2 pre-existing
warnings in unrelated files).
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5

content.text:
Pushed `a20ce1a` to PR #71.

**Addressed (9 review items):**

| Severity | Item | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| Critical | kysely 0.27.0 vuln | Bumped to 0.28.17 to match rest of monorepo |
| Major | `clock.ts` sleep doesn't handle pre-aborted signal | Check `signal.aborted` first, resolve immediately |
| Major | Missing DB CHECK constraints | Added `silence_max >= 0` and `last_status IN (...)` |
| Major | Empty `turnId` would drop wrong turn | Skip drop-turn entirely when turnId missing/empty (real wire schema has no turnId field today) |
| Major | `parent: null` will fail against real git backend | Documented the limitation; correct fix requires workspace contract change (deferred follow-up) |
| Major | "Shared routine reuses conversation" didn't verify reuse | Find-or-create mock caches by externalKey; canary asserts both invokes saw same ctx.conversationId |
| Major | Swallowed errors in DB cleanup | Dropped `.catch(() => {})` from TRUNCATE / client.end() in service-hooks and canary tests |
| Minor | drop-turn no-op test didn't assert contract | `expect(getLastApplied()).toBeUndefined()` |
| Minor | `TIME_OF_DAY_RE` allowed 24:30 | Regex now accepts 00:00–23:59 plus the 24:00 end-of-day sentinel only |

**Deferred (explained in commit message):**
- Cross-plugin `@ax/validator-routine` import — already resolved via the eslint allowlist update (`86e1a7b`).
- `pg_try_advisory_lock` + Kysely pool connection issue — the advisory lock is best-effort election; `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` in `claimDue` is the actual correctness guarantee. Pinning via `db.connection()` is a worthwhile follow-up but doesn't affect Phase B correctness.

Build clean; routines 58/58, conversations 120/120, validator-routine 22/22 green; lint 0 errors.

content.text:
You're right to call this out. The pattern I'm following is too narrow: I ran tests only for packages I modified (`routines`, `conversations`, `validator-routine`, `preset-k8s`) plus `pnpm build`. I never ran the full `pnpm test`. CI catches what I miss.

Let me investigate this specific failure first, then propose a workflow fix.

The `bundle-hooks.test.ts` `slice` error on this PR is the same family of flake: isomorphic-git racing on pack-file / .idx writes under Linux CI's temp filesystem.

Let me first verify nothing real is broken by running the full suite locally, then trigger a CI rerun.

content.text:
Rerun triggered. Now the honest answer to your question.

## Why this keeps happening

**Three things, in order of how much I can control:**

**1. My workflow gap (the one I can fix).** I ran tests only for packages I touched (`routines`, `conversations`, `validator-routine`, `preset-k8s`) plus `pnpm build`. I never ran `pnpm test`. If a downstream package fails deterministically on my changes, I miss it. **I should run `pnpm test` before every push.** No excuse — it's a 60-second cost to save a CI round-trip.

**2. The repo has a chronically flaky test family (not my changes).** Pattern:
- This PR's CI: `workspace-git/bundle-hooks.test.ts` — null buffer in isomorphic-git's pack-index reader
- Recent main CI: `ipc-core/walk.test.ts` — `ENOTEMPTY: rmdir '/tmp/.../objects/pack'`
- This PR's local full-suite run just now: `workspace-git-server/argv-injection.test.ts` (len-64k variant)

All three are integration tests that spawn the real `git` binary against `/tmp`, and they race on pack-file / .idx writes under Linux's filesystem timing. None are caused by Phase B. Running `pnpm test` locally on macOS won't catch them because the race only fires on Linux CI.

**3. The proper fix is repo-level, not PR-level.** Either:
- CI should retry-on-failure for the known-flaky test files (mark them with `vitest --retry=2` or similar)
- Or someone fixes the underlying races (likely `fs.mkdtemp` + cleanup ordering in workspace-git/ipc-core/workspace-git-server)

Until then, every PR has a non-trivial chance of catching one of these on first CI run. Rerunning usually passes them.

## What I'll do differently

- **Always run `pnpm test` locally before pushing.** Catches deterministic regressions even if it can't catch Linux-only flakes.
- **When CI fails on a test I didn't touch, check the failing-test name against the known-flake family before assuming it's my bug.** If it's a known flake, retry; if it's new, investigate.
- **Surface the flake pattern in memory** so future sessions don't re-derive this.

Want me to save the flaky-test-family pattern to project memory so I (and future agents) treat these as known flakes by default?
Let me find the actual race condition.

Now I'll add a binary-safe variant and rewrite `readSnapshotAt`:

lines: -  // Phase B (2026-05-14). Routines core — interval/cron scheduling, plus
-  // silence-token logic via Phase A's conversations:hide / drop-turn /
+  // Phase B (2026-05-14). validator-routine is a pure subscriber and works
+  // anywhere workspace:pre-apply fires — load in local CLI dev too so
+  // routine-file authors get the same veto feedback as production.
+  // @ax/routines itself is NOT loaded here: its init() calls
+  // The local CLI uses `@ax/storage-sqlite` (different mechanism) and
+  // doesn't load the conversations / agents / chat-orchestrator plugins
+  // routines depends on. Loading it would crash bootstrap with
+  // The k8s preset loads it (postgres is present there); see
+  // presets/k8s/src/index.ts and the Phase B canary describe block in
// Sandbox. Config only admits 'subprocess' today; the switch is future-
// proofing for when alternate sandbox providers land.

input.command:
git add -A && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(workspace-git-core): use real git for readSnapshotAt; drop routines from cli local mode

Two bugs were masking each other.

1. readSnapshotAt's isomorphic-git path flakes on CI. The trace:
     loadPackIndex → fs.read(filename) returns null
                   → GitPackIndex.fromIdx({ idx: null })
                   → BufferCursor.slice(null)
                   → "Cannot read properties of null (reading 'slice')"
   isomorphic-git's node-fs adapter (node_modules/.../index.cjs:5021)
   coalesces EVERY I/O error (ENOENT, EAGAIN, EMFILE, EBUSY, partial
   reads, EINTR) into a single `null` return. Under CI load the
   ENOENT pathway fires when readdir lists a .idx that vanishes by
   the time fs.read runs (real git's pack-maintenance can rename or
   consolidate packs between scan and read).

   Fix: use the real `git` binary (ls-tree + cat-file) for snapshot
   reads. Real git handles its own pack files atomically. Adds a
   `runGitBinary` helper (the existing `runGit` lossily utf8-decodes
   stdout, which would corrupt non-text blobs); `readSnapshotAt`
   shells out to `git ls-tree -r -z --name-only <commit>` for paths
   and `git cat-file blob <commit>:<path>` for bytes.

   This is the same bug family flaking workspace-git-server/argv-
   injection and ipc-core/walk in recent main CI runs — same root
   cause (concurrent git ops + isomorphic-git's silently-null reads
   under load).

2. @ax/routines in the local CLI never worked. Phase B Task 16
   wired it into packages/cli/src/main.ts, but the local CLI uses
   @ax/storage-sqlite (which does not register database:get-instance)
   and doesn't load @ax/conversations / @ax/agents / @ax/chat-
   orchestrator at all. The routines plugin's init() calls
   bus.call('database:get-instance', ...) which crashes bootstrap
   with "no plugin registered for service hook 'database:get-
   instance'" — surfaced by four cli e2e tests once the workspace-
   git flake was fixed.

   Fix: remove createRoutinesPlugin() from cli main.ts (and the
   workspace dep + tsconfig ref). The k8s preset still loads it
   (postgres is present there); the preset test still asserts both
   plugins load there. @ax/validator-routine remains in cli — it's
   a pure workspace:pre-apply subscriber with no DB dependency, so
   routine-file authors get the same veto feedback in local dev as
   production.

Verification: full `pnpm test` clean (all packages green, including
the previously-flaking workspace-git/bundle-hooks.test.ts, workspace-
git-server/argv-injection.test.ts, and cli e2e tests). `pnpm build`
clean. `pnpm lint` 0 errors.
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5

content.text:
Pushed `c620e0b`. Two real bugs fixed (not just the flake):

**1. The flake itself** — `readSnapshotAt` now uses real `git ls-tree` + `git cat-file` instead of isomorphic-git's pack walker. isomorphic-git's node-fs adapter coalesces every I/O error (ENOENT, EAGAIN, EMFILE, partial reads) into a single `null` return, which then crashes in `GitPackIndex.fromIdx → BufferCursor.slice(null)`. Real git handles its own pack files atomically. Added a `runGitBinary` helper since the existing `runGit` utf8-decodes stdout (would corrupt non-text blobs).

**2. A bug the flake was hiding** — `@ax/routines` wired into CLI's local mode was never going to work. CLI uses `@ax/storage-sqlite` and doesn't load `@ax/conversations` / `@ax/agents` / `@ax/chat-orchestrator`. The routines plugin's `init()` calls `database:get-instance` (postgres-only) and would crash bootstrap. Once the workspace-git flake was fixed, four CLI e2e tests surfaced this. Removed `createRoutinesPlugin()` from `packages/cli/src/main.ts` (kept `createValidatorRoutinePlugin()` — it's DB-less). The k8s preset is unaffected; its test still asserts both plugins load there.

Full `pnpm test` is now green across the entire monorepo. The previously-flaking `workspace-git/bundle-hooks.test.ts`, `workspace-git-server/argv-injection.test.ts`, and the four CLI e2e tests all pass.

input.command:
git add -A && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(routines,validator-routine): bump vitest + testcontainers to clear 7 transitive advisories

All 7 vulnerabilities flagged in PR #71's audit trace through these
two packages' devDependencies — the rest of the monorepo is already
on the patched versions, so Phase B's package.json files were the
only outliers.

Before / after:
  - @testcontainers/postgresql: ^10.10.0 → 11.14.0
      transitively bumps `undici` past 6.24.0, clearing 5 advisories:
        - GHSA-vrm6-8vpv-qv8q (WebSocket permessage-deflate memory)
        - GHSA-v9p9-hfj2-hcw8 (WebSocket server_max_window_bits crash)
        - GHSA-g9mf-h72j-4rw9 (fetch Content-Encoding decompression)
        - GHSA-2mjp-6q6p-2qxm (HTTP request smuggling)
        - GHSA-4992-7rv2-5pvq (CRLF injection via upgrade option)
  - vitest: ^1.6.0 → ^4.1.4
      transitively bumps `vite` past 6.4.2 and `esbuild` past 0.25.0,
      clearing the remaining 2:
        - GHSA-67mh-4wv8-2f99 (esbuild dev-server CORS)
        - GHSA-4w7w-66w2-5vf9 (vite optimized-deps .map path traversal)

`pnpm audit` now reports "No known vulnerabilities found". `pnpm
build` clean, `pnpm --filter @ax/routines --filter @ax/validator-
routine test` 80/80 green.

The Phase B plan pinned vitest@1 + testcontainers@10 by oversight —
both are far behind the rest of the monorepo. Should have caught
this in Task 4's scaffold instead of letting the audit step on CI
flag it post-merge.
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5
